source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in square $ABCD$, $AB = 4$, point $M$ is on side $CD$, and $DM = 1$. Triangles $AEM$ and $ADM$ are symmetric about the line containing $AM$. Rotating $\triangle ADM$ $90^\circ$ clockwise around point $A$ results in $\triangle ABF$. When connecting $EF$, what is the length of segment $EF$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} \\
Since $\triangle AEM$ and $\triangle ADM$ are symmetrical about the line containing AM,\\
therefore $\triangle AEM \cong \triangle ADM$\\
thus $AE=AD=AB=4$\\
Connect BM, as shown in the figure,\\
since $\triangle ADM$ is rotated clockwise by $90^\circ$ around point A to get $\triangle ABF$,\\
therefore $\triangle ABF \cong \triangle ADM$\\
thus $AF=AM$, $\angle FAB=\angle MAD$\\
thus $\angle FAB=\angle MAE$\\
therefore $\angle FAB+\angle BAE=\angle MAE + \angle BAE$\\
therefore $\angle FAE=\angle MAB$\\
thus $\triangle FAE\cong \triangle MAB$ (SAS)\\
thus $EF=BM$\\
In the square ABCD,\\
therefore $BC=CD=AB=4$\\
since $DM=1$\\
therefore $CM=3$\\
In $\triangle BCM$,\\
$BM=\sqrt{3^2+4^2}=5$\\
therefore $EF=\boxed{5}$

Provided answer includes an explicit derivation ending with EF=5, enabling rule-based or symbolic verification

variants.expected_answer: Since $\triangle AEM$ and $\triangle ADM$ are symmetrical about the line containing $AM$, therefore $\triangle AEM \cong \triangle ADM$, thus $AE=AD=AB=4$. Connect $BM$. Since $\triangle ADM$ is rotated clockwise by $90^\circ$ around point $A$ to get $\triangle ABF$, therefore $\triangle ABF \cong \triangle ADM$, thus $AF=AM$, $\angle FAB=\angle MAD$, and hence $\angle FAB=\angle MAE$. Therefore $\angle FAE=\angle MAB$. Thus $\triangle FAE\cong \triangle MAB$ (SAS), so $EF=BM$. In square $ABCD$, $BC=CD=AB=4$. Since $DM=1$, therefore $CM=3$. In $\triangle BCM$, $BM=\sqrt{3^2+4^2}=5$. Therefore $EF=5$.

open_ended_problem_variants.title: MM-Math 开放题
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, in square $ABCD$, $AB = 4$, point $M$ is on side $CD$, and $DM = 1$. Triangles $AEM$ and $ADM$ are symmetric about the line containing $AM$. Rotating $\triangle ADM$ $90^\circ$ clockwise around point $A$ results in $\triangle ABF$. When connecting $EF$, what is the length of segment $EF$?
open_ended_problem_variants.expected_answer: Since $\triangle AEM$ and $\triangle ADM$ are symmetrical about the line containing $AM$, therefore $\triangle AEM \cong \triangle ADM$, thus $AE=AD=AB=4$. Connect $BM$. Since $\triangle ADM$ is rotated clockwise by $90^\circ$ around point $A$ to get $\triangle ABF$, therefore $\triangle ABF \cong \triangle ADM$, thus $AF=AM$, $\angle FAB=\angle MAD$, and hence $\angle FAB=\angle MAE$. Therefore $\angle FAE=\angle MAB$. Thus $\triangle FAE\cong \triangle MAB$ (SAS), so $EF=BM$. In square $ABCD$, $BC=CD=AB=4$. Since $DM=1$, therefore $CM=3$. In $\triangle BCM$, $BM=\sqrt{3^2+4^2}=5$. Therefore $EF=5$.
